problem_statement: The input primarily provides a project title. A detailed problem statement describing current challenges, inefficiencies, or the specific need this tracker aims to address is currently missing. It's assumed the project aims to streamline the process of recording and managing employee attendance.
solution_overview: The input only provides a project title. A comprehensive solution overview describing the proposed system's main features, architecture, and how it will address the problem statement is currently missing.
functional_requirements: No specific functional requirements have been provided in the input.
Further details are needed to define functionalities such as: employee check-in/check-out methods, attendance record viewing, reporting, leave management integration, etc.
non_functional_requirements: No specific non-functional requirements have been provided in the input.
Further details are needed to define aspects such as: scalability, performance, security, usability, maintainability, and reliability.
tech_stack_recommendation: The input is insufficient to provide a meaningful tech stack recommendation.

Recommendations would depend on factors like: required scale, existing infrastructure, budget, team expertise, integration needs, and specific functional/non-functional requirements. Common considerations for such an application might include: a web-based interface (e.g., React, Angular, Vue), a backend API (e.g., Python/Django, Node.js/Express, Java/Spring Boot, C#/ASP.NET Core), and a database (e.g., PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server).
